senses.definition: to reluctantly pay a significant amount of money

examples.x: I had to fork out a substantial sum for the car repairs, and it was a financial hit.
She had to fork out a considerable amount for her child's tuition fees.
The unexpected medical bills forced him to fork out a large portion of his savings.

senses.definition: to assign a task or project to an external party, typically for a fee

examples.x: The small business decided to farm out its accounting tasks to a professional firm.
Instead of hiring new staff, the company chose to farm out the IT support services.
The construction company farms out specific construction phases to specialized contractors.
The software development team farms out certain coding tasks to freelance programmers.
Recognizing their limitations, the startup decided to farm out the design of their logo to a creative agency.

senses.definition: to distribute something among a group of individuals

examples.x: The charity organization plans to give out food to the needy during the holiday season.
The teacher will give out textbooks to the students at the beginning of the school year.
The coach gives out uniforms to the sports team before the big game.

senses.definition: to provide someone or each person in a group with something

examples.x: She will hand out the pamphlets to the attendees before the seminar.
He handed out flyers in the neighborhood last week.

senses.definition: to help someone, especially to make it easier for them to do something

examples.x: I can help out with your homework if you're having trouble.
She asked her neighbor to help out by watching her pets while she was on vacation.
By this time next week, I will be helping out at the new office.

senses.definition: to rent something to someone, typically for a specified period, in exchange for payment

examples.x: The local car rental agency will hire out vehicles for business trips.
The company offers to hire out conference rooms for corporate meetings.
He plans to hire out his spare bedroom to students looking for accommodation.
The equipment rental shop can hire out construction tools for short-term use.

senses.definition: to distribute something to a group of people

examples.x: The teacher passed out the assignments to the students.
They were passing out flyers for the upcoming event at the mall.
At the end of the seminar, they passed out informational brochures to all attendees.
The organization passed out pamphlets raising awareness about the environmental crisis.

senses.definition: to provide services or temporary use of something to someone, in exchange for a fee

examples.x: Many people in the city rent out their parking spaces to commuters looking for a spot.
The company is willing to rent out its conference rooms for business meetings.

senses.definition: to send something to a number of people or places

examples.x: They sent out newsletters to subscribers to keep them updated on the latest news.
The charity organization sent out relief packages to disaster-stricken areas.
The department head sent out a memo to inform the entire team about the new project.
The company sent out product samples to potential customers to promote their new line.

senses.definition: to divide and allocate a resource, task, or item among individuals

examples.x: The manager decided to share out the responsibilities among the team members.
They are planning to share out the budget for the different project phases.
The organization aims to share out the benefits of the initiative with all participants.
The charity's goal is to share out the donations to reach as many beneficiaries as possible.
